mapper.select: select distinct p.permission_code from user_roles ur inner join roles r on r.id = ur.role_id inner join sys_role_permissions rp on rp.role_id = r.id inner join sys_permissions p on p.id = rp.permission_id where ur.user_id = #{userId} and p.status = 'ENABLED'

select b.id, b.permission_id, p.permission_code, b.http_method, b.path_pattern, b.controller_class, b.handler_method, b.status from sys_api_permission_bindings b inner join sys_permissions p on p.id = b.permission_id where b.http_method = #{httpMethod} and b.path_pattern = #{pathPattern} and b.status = 'ENABLED' and p.status = 'ENABLED' limit 1

select distinct m.id, m.module_id, sm.module_code, sm.module_name, sm.icon as module_icon, sm.sort_order as module_sort_order, m.parent_id, m.menu_code, m.menu_name, m.route_path, m.component_key, m.icon, m.sort_order, m.status from user_roles ur inner join sys_role_permissions rp on rp.role_id = ur.role_id inner join sys_permissions p on p.id = rp.permission_id inner join sys_menus m on m.id = p.menu_id inner join sys_modules sm on sm.id = m.module_id where ur.user_id = #{userId} and p.permission_type = 'PAGE' and p.status = 'ENABLED' and m.status = 'ENABLED' and sm.status = 'ENABLED' order by sm.sort_order asc, sm.id asc, m.sort_order asc, m.id asc
